body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: Reflecting on the full academic year in the Public Relations program at the School of Communication, I can say with confidence that this has been an outstanding and transformative educational experience that has exceeded my expectations in most meaningful ways. The combination of rigorous coursework, dedicated faculty, strong peer community, and access to industry connections has equipped me with both the technical competencies and professional mindset needed to succeed in my chosen field. I have grown significantly as a learner and feel genuinely prepared for the next stage of my academic or professional journey. I applaud the institution for maintaining high standards throughout and look forward to continuing my studies here with great enthusiasm.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Communication has been outstanding and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I recognize the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The curriculum for my Public Relations program at the School of Communication is well-designed and clearly aligned with current industry demands and emerging professional trends in the field. Course structures are logical, building progressively on foundational concepts before introducing advanced topics, which gives students a solid intellectual scaffold. The balance between theoretical foundations and practical application is impressive, with project-based assessments, case studies, and laboratory work that directly reflect real-world professional scenarios I will encounter in my career. I particularly value the inclusion of contemporary tools and technologies, which ensures graduates are competitive and well-prepared for the demands of the modern job market.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: Extracurricular opportunities at the School of Communication, managed by the Student Affairs Office, are disappointing and poorly promoted, limiting student engagement with the broader campus community significantly. The range of active student clubs is narrow, with minimal support for students wishing to establish new interest groups or activities that reflect the diversity of the student population. Events are inconsistently organized and inadequately funded, resulting in low attendance and limited impact on the student community's sense of belonging and engagement. I strongly recommend the Student Affairs Office to take a more proactive and well-resourced approach to extracurricular programming, recognizing its critical role in student retention and overall satisfaction.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: Career preparation support from the Career Center at the School of Communication has been inconsistent and has left many students — including myself — feeling ill-equipped for the competitive graduate employment market. Career workshops are infrequent, poorly promoted, and often scheduled at times that conflict with lecture commitments, reducing student participation. The internship database is outdated and contains many positions that are no longer active, and support for students during internship placements is minimal to nonexistent. I point out the university to substantially reinvest in the Career Center — expanding its employer network, modernizing its tools, and increasing the frequency and quality of career development programming across all academic programs and year levels.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: The financial services managed by the Finance Department at the School of Communication have been a source of insufficient experiences for me and several of my peers this semester, and I feel it is important to communicate these concerns formally. Scholarship disbursements were delayed by over six weeks this semester without advance notice or clear explanation to affected students, creating significant financial hardship for those who rely on these payments. Fee statements are sometimes inaccurate, requiring multiple correction requests that take considerable time and follow-up effort to resolve. I strongly flag the Finance Department to implement automated disbursement tracking, improve error-checking processes, and establish proactive communication protocols when delays are anticipated.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure managed by the IT Support Department presents inadequate challenges that significantly disrupt the academic experience at the School of Communication. Wi-Fi connectivity is unreliable in several key areas of the campus, including the main library, multiple lecture halls, and postgraduate study rooms, making online coursework and research frustrating and inefficient. Computer labs frequently have outdated or malfunctioning equipment, and software licensing issues occasionally prevent students from accessing tools required for coursework. The IT helpdesk response time is problematic, often taking several days to resolve straightforward technical issues. Urgent investment in infrastructure upgrades and improved support staffing is needed.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: The learning materials provided for several of my Public Relations courses at the School of Communication are inconsistent and fall short of what students need to succeed academically. Required textbooks are outdated and frequently out of stock in both the campus bookstore and the library, forcing students to source materials independently at considerable expense and inconvenience. The Learning Management System is inconsistently used by lecturers — some post materials days after class, and some modules have no digital resources whatsoever. I strongly urge the Library Services and IT Support Department to collaborate on ensuring all course resources are accessible digitally, current, and available before the semester commences.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: Academic administrative processes at the School of Communication are satisfactory in their execution, meeting basic requirements but leaving clear room for improvement in both speed and user-friendliness. The online student portal functions adequately for routine tasks such as registration and fee payment, but navigating more complex requests — such as grade appeals or course exemptions — remains unnecessarily cumbersome. Communication timelines are inconsistent, with some requests handled promptly and others taking weeks without updates or acknowledgment. Streamlining administrative workflows, improving the student portal's UX, and setting clear service-level response standards would meaningfully reduce administrative friction for the entire student body.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the Public Relations student experience at the School of Communication center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only acceptable outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
